_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to retell stories we know — through play, acting, drawing and writing.

_source.w: We are learning to retell stories we know — through play, acting, drawing and writing.
y: Retelling stories shows we understood them and helps us remember them.
When we retell in creative ways, we practise being storytellers ourselves.
Adapting stories through play and performance builds our imagination and confidence.
eg: We draw pictures of characters and events from a favourite story and explain our drawings.
We role-play a well-known story using costumes and props.
We retell a familiar story to a partner using our own words.
We use puppets or small figures to act out the main events of a story.
We draw a comic strip that retells a story in three or four panels.
We change part of a story — like giving it a different ending — and share our version with the class.
